Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Terraform-related communications using search_emails (query: "Terraform" or "Rachel Nguyen" or "Vantage Distributed"). Find Rachel Nguyen's relocation request email (rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com) listing three employees needing full relocations: Priya Dasgupta (Sr. Data Engineer, Austin TX to Chicago IL, move-in May 5, has a domestic shorthair cat needing pet-friendly housing), Marcus Liu (Product Designer, Portland OR to New York NY, move-in around May 12, has home studio audio equipment), and Samantha Obi (DevOps Engineer, Denver CO to San Francisco CA, move-in May 8, no special requirements). Also find Rachel's follow-up email with full employee details including current addresses and housing preferences. Also find Catalina's apology email and Mina's introduction email to Rachel acknowledging the pricing incident.
OE 2: Search CRM for the Terraform account using crm_search_companies (name: "Terraform Digital" or "Vantage Distributed"). Find company record company-terraform-digital (display name: "Vantage Distributed", type: CUSTOMER, 1,200 employees). Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company-terraform-digital"). Find two deals: deal-terraform-digital-001 ($1,850,000, closedwon, the original enterprise contract) and deal-terraform-q2-reloc-batch1 ($7,500, stage: qualification, AM: Mina Hashimoto, 3 employees with May move dates). List CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company-terraform-digital"]). Find engagement-terraform-replyall-incident documenting the full reply-all pricing leak: Catalina exposed 25% margin floor, tiered volume discount structure (8% for 11-20 moves, 12% for 21+), Rachel locked in tiered discount as her expectation.
OE 3: Search Slack for Terraform and pricing leak discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Terraform" or "Reply-All" or "pricing leak"). Find David Chen's escalation in #customer-engagement about Catalina accidentally Reply-All'ing internal pricing to Rachel Nguyen. Find Elena's directive assigning Mina as account owner. Find David's follow-up confirming the 48-hour proposal deadline and instructing Mina to coordinate with Marcus and Alejandro on volume pricing tiers before the proposal goes out.
OE 4: Search Airtable for existing Terraform relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: "Company", value: "Terraform Digital"). Find NO individual relocation records for Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, or Samantha Obi. Only find a client account record (recTerraformDigital01) in the Client Accounts table showing Terraform Digital, AM: Mina Hashimoto, Status: Onboarding. Nothing has been set up in the relocation tracking system.
OE 5: Search Linear for Terraform tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Terraform" or "Vantage"). Find NO tracking tickets for any of the three Terraform employee relocations. Only find one indirect ticket: "Invoice email template safety checks, prevent attachment mismatches" (#187, state: backlog, assignee: Lena Bjorkstrom), created after the pricing leak but not about the relocations.
OE 6: Search emails for the Canopy executive relocation using search_emails (query: "Mehta" or "Patricia Langford" or "executive relocation"). Find Patricia Langford's original request (patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) for Dr. Anand Mehta's CMO relocation from Boston to Chicago, on-site by May 5. Discover the coordinator by finding Fatimah Al-Rashidi's introduction emails to Patricia and Dr. Mehta (CC'd to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com), which lay out three workstreams: (a) temporary housing, (b) chain-of-custody for medical equipment (2x Eizo RadiForce RX660 diagnostic monitors at ~$12K each, SentrySafe DH-109E filing cabinet ~350 lbs with HIPAA-sensitive patient research data), (c) household move via Swift Relocations. Also find Dr. Mehta's equipment inventory response with serial numbers (RX660-1147A, RX660-1203A) and Fatimah's three temp housing options from UrbanNest: Option A River North (The Langham) $1,250/week, Option B Streeterville (The Pinnacle) $1,350/week, Option C Gold Coast (The Elysian) $1,450/week.
OE 7: Search CRM for the Mehta deal using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_canopy" or dealname: "Mehta"). Find deal crm_deal_c7ed21dc7fdb, "Canopy Health, Dr. Anand Mehta Executive Relocation (Boston to Chicago, May 2026)", amount: $12,170, stage: inprogress, coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, AM: Mina Hashimoto. Description states "FIRST C-SUITE PLACEMENT FOR CANOPY HEALTH." Estimate reference: EST-2026-0422 at $12,170 total. Also find engagement crm_engagement_ac101931ed37 documenting the account update by Mina on April 23.
OE 8: Search Linear for Mehta tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Mehta" or "Canopy" or "chain-of-custody"). Find three active P1 tickets all assigned to Fatimah: (a) #343 "Temporary Housing" (in_progress, due May 1, seeking 2BR furnished River North/Streeterville), (b) #375 "Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody" (in_progress, due April 30, covers Eizo monitors and SentrySafe cabinet), (c) #396 "Household Move" (in_progress, due May 3, full household via Swift Relocations). All three confirm Fatimah is actively working on the move.
OE 9: Search QuickBooks for the Mehta estimate using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: "Mehta" or "Canopy"). Find estimate est_canopy_mehta_exec_reloc, total: $12,170, status: Pending, due: May 7. Verify the 7 line items: apartment sourcing $1,000, temp housing 4 weeks $4,800, chain-of-custody surcharge $1,200, specialty equipment crating x2 $800, flight booking 2 passengers (premium cabin) $370, household move $3,500, White-Glove Executive Service Premium $500. Cross-check against the CRM deal amount ($12,170) to confirm the numbers match. The prompt asks whether the numbers quoted to Patricia line up with what is in QuickBooks.
OE 10: Search emails for the StormCloud recovery plan using search_emails (query: "Liam Park" or "StormCloud" or "recovery plan"). Find Mina's recovery plan email (sender: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, to: liam.park@stormcloud.io) listing specific commitments: (a) reopen Jae-won Kim housing and send guarantor packet to UrbanNest, (b) correct the $500 goodwill credit onto the live invoice, (c) provide status on Simone Richter's stipend reimbursement, (d) recover the Ridgeline Robotics referral. Also find Liam Park's earlier escalation email (liam.park@stormcloud.io, CFO) listing the same four items as unresolved. The agent must identify these four items from the recovery plan email to know what to verify.
OE 11: Search emails for the Jae-won Kim housing issue using search_emails (query: "Jae-won" or "guarantor" or "Barton Creek"). Find Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest flagged the missing corporate guarantor packet on April 8. Find Suki Patel replied April 9 promising to send by end of week but never followed through. Find Chloe Vance's ops audit confirming the packet was never sent. Find Jae-won's April 15 email to support asking for lease documents. Search Airtable using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: "Name", value: "Jae-won Kim"). Find record recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin showing Status: In Progress, coordinator: Suki Patel, move Seattle WA to Austin TX. Jae-won is still in temporary housing with no signed permanent lease.
OE 12: Search Slack for the goodwill credit using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "goodwill credit" or "StormCloud flight"). Find Mina's email to Tom Wexler confirming a $500 goodwill credit was issued after a flight booking mistake. Find Marcus Thorne's April 25 post confirming the credit was applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the live invoice, so from StormCloud's perspective the credit never showed up. Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: "StormCloud"). Find inv_stormcloud_01 ($26,400, full balance remaining) confirming the credit is not reflected.
OE 13: Search emails for Simone Richter's reimbursement using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "standing desk"). Find Simone's email (simone.richter@stormcloud.io) from April 16 about a $340 standing desk claim submitted March 25 still showing Pending Review. Find Mina's forward to Marcus Thorne asking to release it. Find Marcus's response denying the request: finance is holding ALL pending stipend reimbursements until the fraud review is complete regardless of client or amount.
OE 14: Search emails for the Ridgeline referral using search_emails (query: "Ridgeline" or "Kenji Watanabe"). Find Anya Petrova's referral email (anya.petrova@stormcloud.io) introducing Kenji Watanabe (kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com, HR Director, Ridgeline Robotics) for an Austin relocation batch of 5 employees. Find Kenji's follow-up email that went unread (is_read: false). Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline Robotics"). Find company crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2 (Prospect) and deal crm_deal_a95b4721982a ($8,750, stage: Qualified To Buy), both created April 24 during recovery. Find David's belated response to Kenji on April 24. The referral was initially dropped but partially recovered.
OE 15: Search Linear for StormCloud tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won"). Find three tickets: (a) linear_issue_c97359216490 #284, "StormCloud Relocation, Jae-won Kim, SEA to AUS", state: closed (prematurely, despite guarantor still pending), assignee: Suki Patel; (b) linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0 #412, "Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents", priority: 4 (low), assignee: null (unassigned); (c) linear_issue_a49b4d199139, "StormCloud account incident reconstruction", priority: 1 (urgent), assignee: Mina Hashimoto, covering all 4 recovery items.
OE 16: Look up contact details for Rachel Nguyen using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Rachel Nguyen"). Find rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com. Look up Mina Hashimoto using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto"). Find mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
OE 17: Create Airtable relocation records for the three Terraform employees using airtable_create_record (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations). Create three records: (a) fields: Name: Priya Dasgupta, Company: Terraform Digital, Origin: Austin TX, Destination: Chicago IL, Move Start Date: 2026-05-05, Special Requirements: pet-friendly housing (cat named Byte), Status: In Progress; (b) fields: Name: Marcus Liu, Company: Terraform Digital, Origin: Portland OR, Destination: New York NY, Move Start Date: 2026-05-12, Special Requirements: home studio with audio equipment, Status: In Progress; (c) fields: Name: Samantha Obi, Company: Terraform Digital, Origin: Denver CO, Destination: San Francisco CA, Move Start Date: 2026-05-08, Status: In Progress.
OE 18: Create Linear tracking tickets for unresolved StormCloud recovery items using linear_create_issue (team: team_operations). Create tickets for items that are not yet fully resolved: (a) title: "StormCloud, Jae-won Kim guarantor packet still not sent, Austin lease unsigned" with description covering the guarantor paperwork and temp housing status; (b) title: "StormCloud, $500 goodwill credit applied to wrong document, needs correction to live invoice" with description of the misapplication; (c) title: "StormCloud, Simone Richter $340 desk reimbursement frozen under blanket stipend hold" with description of the status and Marcus's denial.
OE 19: Update CRM deal notes on all three accounts using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE") for each. (a) Terraform Digital (company_ids: ["company-terraform-digital"]): document Q2 batch status, no relocation records existed, now created, Rachel's pricing expectations from reply-all incident, proposal deadline; (b) Canopy Health (company_ids: ["company_canopy"]): document Dr. Mehta executive relocation status, coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi managing 3 workstreams, estimate EST-2026-0422 issued at $12,170, QuickBooks line items verified, housing options presented; (c) StormCloud Analytics (company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]): document recovery plan verification with per-item status, Jae-won housing still unresolved, goodwill credit misapplied, Simone reimbursement frozen, Ridgeline referral partially recovered.
OE 20: Send email to Rachel Nguyen using send_email (sender: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, recipients: ["rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com"]). Email subject references introduction and relocation scope. Body includes: Mina's introduction as dedicated AM, acknowledgment of three employees (Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, Samantha Obi), confirmation of target move dates (May 5, May 12, May 8), preliminary scope overview for each move, and next steps on timing and pricing.
OE 21: Post status update in #customer-engagement using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C002). Update covers all three accounts: (a) Terraform Digital: new account onboarded, 3 relocation records created, proposal in progress, pricing context from reply-all incident; (b) Canopy Health: Dr. Mehta executive relocation on track, coordinator identified as Fatimah, 3 workstreams in progress, estimate issued and verified; (c) StormCloud Analytics: recovery plan verification shows multiple items still unresolved, tracking tickets created for follow-up.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Terraform-related communications using search_emails (query: "Terraform" or "Rachel Nguyen" or "Vantage Distributed"). Find Rachel Nguyen's relocation request email (rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com) listing three employees needing full relocations: Priya Dasgupta (Sr. Data Engineer, Austin TX to Chicago IL, move-in May 5, has a domestic shorthair cat needing pet-friendly housing), Marcus Liu (Product Designer, Portland OR to New York NY, move-in around May 12, has home studio audio equipment), and Samantha Obi (DevOps Engineer, Denver CO to San Francisco CA, move-in May 8, no special requirements). Also find Rachel's follow-up email with full employee details including current addresses and housing preferences. Also find Catalina's apology email and Mina's introduction email to Rachel acknowledging the pricing incident.
OE 2: Search CRM for the Terraform account using crm_search_companies (name: "Terraform Digital" or "Vantage Distributed"). Find company record company-terraform-digital (display name: "Vantage Distributed", type: CUSTOMER, 1,200 employees). OE 3: Search Slack for Terraform and pricing leak discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Terraform" or "Reply-All" or "pricing leak"). Find Simone's email (simone.richter@stormcloud.io) from April 16 about a $340 standing desk claim submitted March 25 still showing Pending Review. Find Mina's forward to Marcus Thorne asking to release it. Find Marcus's response denying the request: finance is holding ALL pending stipend reimbursements until the fraud review is complete regardless of client or amount.
OE 14: Search emails for the Ridgeline referral using search_emails (query: "Ridgeline" or "Kenji Watanabe"). Find Anya Petrova's referral email (anya.petrova@stormcloud.io) introducing Kenji Watanabe (kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com, HR Director, Ridgeline Robotics) for an Austin relocation batch of 5 employees. Find Kenji's follow-up email that went unread (is_read: false). Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline Robotics"). Find company crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2 (Prospect) and deal crm_deal_a95b4721982a ($8,750, stage: Qualified To Buy), both created April 24 during recovery. Find David's belated response to Kenji on April 24. The referral was initially dropped but partially recovered.
OE 15: Search Linear for StormCloud tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won"). Find three tickets: (a) linear_issue_c97359216490 #284, "StormCloud Relocation, Jae-won Kim, SEA to AUS", state: closed (prematurely, despite guarantor still pending), assignee: Suki Patel; (b) linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0 #412, "Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents", priority: 4 (low), assignee: null (unassigned); (c) linear_issue_a49b4d199139, "StormCloud account incident reconstruction", priority: 1 (urgent), assignee: Mina Hashimoto, covering all 4 recovery items.
OE 16: Look up contact details for Rachel Nguyen using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Rachel Nguyen"). Find rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com. Look up Mina Hashimoto using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto"). Find mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
OE 17: Create Airtable relocation records for the three Terraform employees using airtable_create_record (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations). Create three records: (a) fields: Name: Priya Dasgupta, Company: Terraform Digital, Origin: Austin TX, Destination: Chicago IL, Move Start Date: 2026-05-05, Special Requirements: pet-friendly housing (cat named Byte), Status: In Progress; (b) fields: Name: Marcus Liu, Company: Terraform Digital, Origin: Portland OR, Destination: New York NY, Move Start Date: 2026-05-12, Special Requirements: home studio with audio equipment, Status: In Progress; (c) fields: Name: Samantha Obi, Company: Terraform Digital, Origin: Denver CO, Destination: San Francisco CA, Move Start Date: 2026-05-08, Status: In Progress.
OE 18: Create Linear tracking tickets for unresolved StormCloud recovery items using linear_create_issue (team: team_operations). Create tickets for items that are not yet fully resolved: (a) title: "StormCloud, Jae-won Kim guarantor packet still not sent, Austin lease unsigned" with description covering the guarantor paperwork and temp housing status; (b) title: "StormCloud, $500 goodwill credit applied to wrong document, needs correction to live invoice" with description of the misapplication; (c) title: "StormCloud, Simone Richter $340 desk reimbursement frozen under blanket stipend hold" with description of the status and Marcus's denial.
OE 19: Update CRM deal notes on all three accounts using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE") for each. (a) Terraform Digital (company_ids: ["company-terraform-digital"]): document Q2 batch status, no relocation records existed, now created, Rachel's pricing expectations from reply-all incident, proposal deadline; (b) Canopy Health (company_ids: ["company_canopy"]): document Dr. Mehta executive relocation status, coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi managing 3 workstreams, estimate EST-2026-0422 issued at $12,170, QuickBooks line items verified, housing options presented; (c) StormCloud Analytics (company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]): document recovery plan verification with per-item status, Jae-won housing still unresolved, goodwill credit misapplied, Simone reimbursement frozen, Ridgeline referral partially recovered.
OE 20: Send email to Rachel Nguyen using send_email (sender: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, recipients: ["rachel.nguyen@terraformdigital.com"]). Email subject references introduction and relocation scope. Body includes: Mina's introduction as dedicated AM, acknowledgment of three employees (Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, Samantha Obi), confirmation of target move dates (May 5, May 12, May 8), preliminary scope overview for each move, and next steps on timing and pricing.
OE 21: Post status update in #customer-engagement using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C002). Update covers all three accounts: (a) Terraform Digital: new account onboarded, 3 relocation records created, proposal in progress, pricing context from reply-all incident; (b) Canopy Health: Dr. Mehta executive relocation on track, coordinator identified as Fatimah, 3 workstreams in progress, estimate issued and verified; (c) StormCloud Analytics: recovery plan verification shows multiple items still unresolved, tracking tickets created for follow-up.

30 The final response confirms whether the QuickBooks estimate for Dr. Mehta's relocation matches the amount quoted to Patricia, referencing the $12,170 total or the individual line items from the estimate.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response confirms the QuickBooks estimate EST-2026-0422 totaling $12,170 and identifies a discrepancy: the housing line was estimated at $1,200/week ($4,800 total) but UrbanNest's actual rates range from $1,250-$1,450/week, making the estimate $200-$600 short.
✓Run #2The final response identifies the EST-2026-0422 estimate at $12,170 and explicitly calls out a $600 discrepancy on the housing line, explaining the actual cost should be ~$12,770 based on real UrbanNest rates. This directly addresses whether the numbers quoted to Patricia align with QuickBooks.
✓Run #3The final response explicitly states 'The estimate (EST-2026-0422, $12,170)' and identifies a specific discrepancy: the housing line is $200-$600 short because UrbanNest options start at $1,250/week vs the $1,200/week budgeted. This confirms the estimate was cross-checked against actual costs.
✓Run #4The final response confirms the QuickBooks estimate EST-2026-0422 at $12,170 with line items listed, and notes a discrepancy: the temp housing line at $1,200/week doesn't match actual options ($1,250-$1,450/week), resulting in a potential $600 shortfall. This directly addresses whether the quoted numbers align with QuickBooks.
✓Run #5The final response confirms the QB estimate EST-2026-0422 at $12,170 and explicitly identifies the temp housing discrepancy ($1,200/wk budgeted vs. $1,250-$1,450/wk actual UrbanNest options), with all other line items checked out (apartment sourcing $1,000, chain-of-custody surcharge $1,200, specialty crating $800, household moving $3,500, flights $370, executive service premium $500).
✓Run #6The final response explicitly states the financial discrepancy: 'The QuickBooks estimate (EST-2026-0422, $12,170) quotes temporary housing at $1,200/week. But UrbanNest's recommended Streeterville option is $1,350/week. Over 4 weeks, that's a $600 gap ($4,800 quoted vs. $5,400 actual).' This cross-checks the estimate against actuals with specific line items and concludes that there is a $600 discrepancy.
